metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes (inline `/model set <name>`); daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

Gemini CLI 0.46.0 ships `/model` as a built-in TUI command (`packages/cli/src/ui/commands/modelCommand.ts`, bundled at chunk-SCXTH56Q.js L77935-77985). It has two sub-commands: `manage` (returns `{type:"dialog",dialog:"model"}` — the arrow-key picker, the default when `/model` is run bare) and **`set`** — `Usage: /model set <model-name> [--persist]`. The `set` action parses its args inline (`parts[0]` = model name, `--persist` optional) and calls `config.setModel(modelName, !persist)`, then prints `Model set to <name>`. This is a DETERMINISTIC INLINE-ARGUMENT slash form — no picker — so the model switch is sendable as a plain `slash` step, exactly analogous to claudecode's `/model sonnet`. The switch takes effect on the next turn, whose `model` field carries the new id (the same field the parser already reads for first-turn identification). Without `--persist` the change is SESSION-ONLY (`setModel(name, /*sessionOnly=*/ !persist)`), so — unlike claudecode, whose inline `/model` writes the global default — gemini needs NO trailing restore step.

### daemon_capability: full

The geminicli parser already does per-turn model attribution and token accumulation, the two signals this scenario asserts:
- ModelName per turn — `parseAssistant` (parser.go L170-172) sets `ev.ModelName = tailer.NormalizeModelName(raw["model"])` on EVERY `type:"gemini"` assistant message. NormalizeModelName leaves gemini ids unchanged (no claude pattern matches). So turn 1's message carries model A and turn 2's carries model B; the MergeMetrics 'latest non-empty/non-unknown wins' rule (session.go L627-628) promotes B to SessionMetrics.ModelName by t2_end. This is the same machinery the already-recorded 1-8 model-context-display cell verified.
- PerTurnContribution.Model — `applyTokens` (parser.go L247, L264) stamps `Model: ev.ModelName` onto each turn's billable contribution, so turn 1's tokens attribute to A and turn 2's to B (per-turn cost attribution).
- TotalTokens accumulates — each assistant message contributes a deduped billable delta (deduped per id to avoid re-billing a streaming re-emission); the two turns' contributions add independently regardless of model identity, so TotalTokens after t2 > after t1.

### model_switch_window (no working flicker)

The `/model set` slash is a local TUI command: it runs `config.setModel` in-process and emits an info line — it does NO LLM round-trip and writes NO `type:"gemini"`/`type:"user"` turn message to the transcript. The parser's `parseMessage` only opens a turn on a `user` text prompt or settles on a `gemini` message; a local-command info line is neither, so no state_transition to working/waiting fires during the switch window — state stays ready, satisfying the window invariant. (Gemini has no `<local-command>` wrapper to filter, unlike claudecode; the slash simply leaves no turn trace at all.)

### driver_capability: ready

The interactive driver's `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"` covers every step this recipe needs. The switch is `/model set <model-name>` driven via the existing `slash` arm (aliased to `send_text` — same keystrokes as `send`), NOT the arrow-key picker — so NO `keys` step and NO driver_gap. The picker path (`manage`) IS a `keys` gap, but `set` sidesteps it entirely.

## Recipe notes (model A determinism)

Unlike claudecode, the gemini driver launches `gemini -y` and IGNORES its settings-path arg ($4) — there is no `--settings`/`--model` flag wired, so model A CANNOT be pinned via settings. Model A for turn 1 is therefore the account's OAuth boot/default model (the 1-8 fixture booted `gemini-3-flash-preview`); turn 2 switches to a DIFFERENT available model via `/model set`. Per issue #440, the durable assertion is that the model CHANGES (A != B) and a non-empty model B is latest at session end — not a hardcoded pair (the exact boot model is account/rollout-dependent). The recipe targets `gemini-2.5-flash` as the switch target B (a stable, broadly-available id distinct from any gemini-3 default boot model); if the account ever boots on 2.5-flash already, record swaps B to another listed id.

The `slash` arm BUMPS the driver's EXPECTED_TURNS counter exactly like `send`, but `/model set` produces no assistant turn — so a `wait_turn` after the slash (or after turn 2, now off-by-one) would block forever. Same compromise as the claudecode/pi/aider model-switch recipes: turn 1 uses `wait_turn` (counter in sync), the `/model set` slash + turn 2 are covered by a fixed sleep (a one-word reply settles working->ready well within the window).

## Provisional point (keeps confidence at 0.8)

`daemon=full` here is derived from reading the parser plus a live transcript that proves the per-turn `model` field shape (a 2026-06-11 session under ~/.gemini/tmp showed `{"model":"gemini-3.5-flash",...}` on a settled gemini turn). What I have NOT confirmed from docs is that `config.setModel(name, sessionOnly)` causes the NEXT request's persisted transcript `model` field to flip to B (vs the SDK echoing the boot model). The behavior is consistent with how the field is sourced from the active config model, but only a live recording with two DISTINCT message.model values can promote this from provisional to settled — that is `record`'s job.
caveats: Model A is NOT pinnable: the gemini driver launches `gemini -y` with no --settings/--model wiring (it ignores $4), so turn 1 runs on the account's OAuth boot model. The recipe asserts model CHANGE (A != B), not a hardcoded A — per issue #440.
Switch target B (`gemini-2.5-flash`) is chosen as a stable id distinct from the gemini-3 default boot model; if the account boots on 2.5-flash, record must pick a different B so A != B holds.

No global-default restore step is needed: `/model set` WITHOUT `--persist` is session-only (`config.setModel(name, !persist)` -> sessionOnly=true), so the recording leaves the user's default untouched — unlike claudecode's inline /model, which writes ~/.claude/settings.json.

Emission is provisional: the per-turn `model` field flipping to B after `/model set` is inferred from the config->request->transcript path and is not stated in docs; record's live capture (two distinct message.model values) is the only thing that settles it.

WHY no wait_turn after the slash or after turn 2: the driver's `slash` arm bumps EXPECTED_TURNS like `send`, but /model set produces no assistant turn, so a following wait_turn would hang. Turn 2 is covered by a fixed sleep instead (same pattern as pi/aider/claudecode model-switch recipes).

sources.note: setModelCommand: `Usage: /model set <model-name> [--persist]`, autoExecute:false, action parses args inline -> config.setModel(modelName, !persist). Deterministic inline-argument slash (no picker); manageModelCommand is the dialog/picker path.
parseAssistant sets ev.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(raw["model"]) on every gemini message and settles to turn_done on non-empty text + 0 toolCalls — per-turn model attribution is built in.
applyTokens stamps PerTurnContribution.Model = ev.ModelName per turn and contributes a deduped billable delta — tokens accumulate across both models (T2 > T1).
MergeMetrics ModelName rule: latest non-empty/non-unknown wins — turn 2's model B replaces turn 1's model A in SessionMetrics by t2_end.
Live transcript: a settled gemini turn carries {"model":"gemini-3.5-flash", tokens:{...}, content non-empty, toolCalls:[]} — proves the per-turn model field shape the parser reads.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"; slash arm (send|slash -> send_text) is implemented and bumps EXPECTED_TURNS. Recipe needs only these four step types -> driver=ready, no keys gap.

Reference cell for this scenario: same spec shape, inline /model switch, latest-wins promotion, no-flicker switch window. gemini differs in: session-only switch (no restore), no model-A pin (boot default).

preconditions: gemini CLI 0.46.0 on PATH and OAuth-authenticated (the account's boot model is model A)
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record; isolated mode)
the switch target model B (`gemini-2.5-flash`) is available on the account AND differs from the OAuth boot model A (if the account boots on 2.5-flash, record picks a different B so A != B)
tmux available (the gemini interactive driver is tmux-REPL based)
setup: Driver launches `gemini -y` under tmux in a fresh cwd (accepts the trust-folder prompt on first run). gemini mints its own sessionId; the driver harvests it from the transcript header. Turn 1 runs on the account's OAuth boot/default model (model A) — there is NO --model/--settings pin available in this driver.

Between turns the driver sends `/model set gemini-2.5-flash` as a single `slash` step (inline argument, NO picker). Without `--persist` the switch is SESSION-ONLY, so no global-default restore is needed afterward. Turn 2 then runs under model B.
Compare ModelName against the NORMALIZED form (gemini ids pass through NormalizeModelName unchanged); assert A != B and B latest at session end, not a hardcoded pair (issue #440).

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: turn-one
Reply with exactly the word: turn-two

verify: events.jsonl shows two ready->working->ready cycles on the same sessionId (one per turn).

transcript carries TWO DISTINCT message.model values — model A (the OAuth boot model) on turn 1's gemini message and model B (gemini-2.5-flash) on turn 2's. This is the load-bearing evidence the switch happened.
events.jsonl carries model_name = <model A> at the t1_end metric snapshot and = <model B> at t2_end (MergeMetrics latest-non-unknown-wins promotes B).
events.jsonl shows NO state_transition to working or waiting during the /model set window between t1_end and t2_start (the inline slash does no LLM round-trip and writes no turn message).
PerTurnContribution.Model for turn 1 = model A; for turn 2 = model B (per-turn cost attribution).
SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after t2_end > TotalTokens after t1_end (accumulates across both models).
the account's default model is UNCHANGED after the run (the switch was session-only — no --persist).
recipe.notes: gemini-cli model switch differs from claudecode in three ways. (1) NO MODEL-A PIN: the gemini interactive driver launches `gemini -y` and ignores its settings-path arg, so there is no --model/--settings flag — turn 1 runs on the account's OAuth boot model (the 1-8 fixture booted gemini-3-flash-preview). Model A is therefore environment-dependent; the durable assertion is A != B and B-latest-at-end, per issue #440. (2) SESSION-ONLY SWITCH, NO RESTORE: `/model set <name>` without `--persist` calls config.setModel(name, sessionOnly=true), so the global default is untouched — there is no trailing `/model set <A>` restore step (claudecode needs one because its inline /model writes settings.json). (3) NO CACHE-CONFIRMATION DIALOG: gemini's `set` sub-command applies directly and prints `Model set to <name>` — there is no one-key 'conversation is cached' confirm dialog (the claudecode mid-session gotcha), so no `keys 1`+`Enter` is needed. WHY NO wait_turn after the slash / after turn 2: the driver's `slash` arm calls send_text which BUMPS EXPECTED_TURNS exactly like send, but /model set produces no assistant turn — so after the slash EXPECTED is off-by-one and a turn-2 wait_turn would block forever. Same compromise as the pi/aider/claudecode model-switch recipes: turn 1 uses wait_turn (counter in sync), the slash + turn 2 are covered by a fixed 15s sleep (a one-word `turn-two` reply completes and settles working->ready well within that window). The model A->B promotion lands via MergeMetrics' latest-non-empty/non-unknown-wins rule (session.go L627-628) and PerTurnContribution.Model is set per-turn in applyTokens. What differs between runs: the exact boot model A, token counts/cost, sessionId/PID/timestamps. What must NOT differ: two distinct message.model values (A != B), two ready->working->ready arcs on one sessionId, no waiting/working during the switch window, and ModelName = B (latest) at t2_end. The switch target B = gemini-2.5-flash; if the account boots on 2.5-flash, record must pick a different B.
